USA.name: USA
USA.fact: Hosting its first men's World Cup since 1994 — a tournament that still holds the record for the highest average attendance in history. This time it stages the final, at MetLife Stadium.

Canada.name: Canada
Canada.fact: Co-hosts at their third men's World Cup — still chasing a first-ever knockout-stage appearance.

Mexico.name: Mexico
Mexico.fact: The only country to host three men's World Cups (1970, 1986 and 2026) — and it kicks the whole thing off against South Africa at the Estadio Azteca.

Argentina.name: Argentina
Argentina.fact: Defending champions — won South American qualifying by nine points with a region-high 31 goals. Lionel Messi, now 38, is chasing back-to-back titles in surely his final World Cup.

Brazil.name: Brazil
Brazil.fact: Record five-time winners and the only nation to have played at every men's World Cup — all 23 of them.

Uruguay.name: Uruguay
Uruguay.fact: Winners of the very first World Cup, in 1930 — and with around 3.4 million people, still the smallest nation ever to lift the trophy.

Colombia.name: Colombia
Colombia.fact: Back after missing the 2022 tournament. Their best ever run (quarter-finals, 2014) came with James Rodríguez winning the Golden Boot — and scoring THAT volley.

Ecuador.name: Ecuador
Ecuador.fact: Conceded only five goals across 18 qualifiers — eight of which ended 0-0 — helped by making visitors play in Quito at 2,850 metres above sea level.

Paraguay.name: Paraguay
Paraguay.fact: Returns after missing the last three World Cups — last seen in 2010, when they reached their first ever quarter-final.

England.name: England
England.fact: Won all eight qualifiers, scoring 22 goals and conceding none. Sixty years since their only title in 1966 — and their first World Cup under a German manager, Thomas Tuchel.

Scotland.name: Scotland
Scotland.fact: A long-awaited return after 28 years away — and in eight previous World Cups they have never once escaped the group stage. Surely this time.

France.name: France
France.fact: The 2022 runners-up, and finalists at the last two World Cups — champions in 2018, beaten only on penalties in 2022.

Spain.name: Spain
Spain.fact: The highest-ranked team in the tournament and reigning European champions — many bookies' favourites to add to their 2010 title.

Portugal.name: Portugal
Portugal.fact: Carries Cristiano Ronaldo back into the tournament — at 41, the first man ever to play at six World Cups. Still never won it. Last chance saloon.

Germany.name: Germany
Germany.fact: Four-time champions seeking redemption after group-stage exits in both 2018 and 2022 — a third would be unthinkable.

Netherlands.name: Netherlands
Netherlands.fact: The strongest footballing nation never to win it: three finals (1974, 1978, 2010), three defeats. No one has lost more.

Belgium.name: Belgium
Belgium.fact: Trying to stay relevant past its 'golden generation' era, which peaked at third place in 2018 — this is the new wave's first crack.

Croatia.name: Croatia
Croatia.fact: A proven tournament overachiever with a population under 4 million — 2018 finalists, third in 2022, and Luka Modrić still going at 40.

Switzerland.name: Switzerland
Switzerland.fact: One of Europe's most reliable group-stage performers — won its qualifying group convincingly and is at a sixth consecutive World Cup, but has never gone past the quarter-finals.

Austria.name: Austria
Austria.fact: Topped its group with 22 goals — ten of them in a single 10-0 win over San Marino. First World Cup since 1998; their best ever finish (third) came back in 1954.

Norway.name: Norway
Norway.fact: First World Cup since 1998, with rare superstar power in Erling Haaland and Martin Ødegaard — eight wins from eight in qualifying, Haaland scoring 16, including an 11-1 win over Moldova. Yes, really.

Bosnia-Herzegovina.name: Bosnia & Herz.
Bosnia-Herzegovina.fact: Only their second ever World Cup, returning for the first time since their 2014 debut — having knocked out four-time champions Italy on penalties in the playoffs.

Sweden.name: Sweden
Sweden.fact: Scraped in via the Nations League playoff path after finishing LAST in its qualifying group with two points — and now arrives with arguably Europe's scariest strike pair in Isak and Gyökeres.

Turkey.name: Türkiye
Turkey.fact: Qualified through the European playoffs, beating Kosovo 1-0 in the final — their first World Cup since 2002, when they stunned everyone by finishing third.

Czechia.name: Czechia
Czechia.fact: First World Cup since 2006 — past Ireland on penalties, then a 3-1 playoff final win over Denmark. As Czechoslovakia they twice reached the final, and twice lost.

Japan.name: Japan
Japan.fact: The first team in the world to qualify for 2026, back in March 2025 — and they beat both Germany and Spain at the last World Cup.

South Korea.name: South Korea
South Korea.fact: Won its third-round qualifying group by six points — an eleventh consecutive World Cup, the longest active streak of any team outside Europe and South America.

Iran.name: Iran
Iran.fact: A fourth straight World Cup — but still seeking a first-ever knockout-stage appearance from six previous attempts.

Australia.name: Australia
Australia.fact: Qualified automatically by finishing second to Japan in its group — a sixth straight World Cup for cricket's old enemy, so absolutely no Saints sympathy if they go out early.

Jordan.name: Jordan
Jordan.fact: Making its World Cup debut after a breakthrough Asian qualifying campaign — they'd never even come close before.

Uzbekistan.name: Uzbekistan
Uzbekistan.fact: Tournament debutant and the first Central Asian nation ever to reach the men's World Cup — three decades in the making since independence.

Qatar.name: Qatar
Qatar.fact: Their first World Cup qualified on merit — they previously appeared only as 2022 hosts, when they lost all three games.

Saudi Arabia.name: Saudi Arabia
Saudi Arabia.fact: Responsible for arguably the greatest shock in World Cup history — beating eventual champions Argentina in their 2022 opener — and won its group in the fourth round of Asian qualifying.

Iraq.name: Iraq
Iraq.fact: Returns for the first time since its sole previous appearance in 1986 — the 48th and final team to qualify, now managed by former Australia boss Graham Arnold.

Morocco.name: Morocco
Morocco.fact: Building on its historic 2022 run as the first African team ever to reach a World Cup semi-final — the whole continent will be behind them again.

Senegal.name: Senegal
Senegal.fact: Went unbeaten in qualifying, conceding just three goals — and famously announced themselves by beating holders France in their very first World Cup match in 2002.

Egypt.name: Egypt
Egypt.fact: Carries Mohamed Salah as its headline star and conceded just two goals in qualifying — yet for all their record seven Africa Cup of Nations titles, Egypt have never won a World Cup knockout game.

Algeria.name: Algeria
Algeria.fact: Won its African group by seven points, losing only once. In 1982 they beat West Germany — then got knocked out by the infamous 'Disgrace of Gijón' stitch-up.

Tunisia.name: Tunisia
Tunisia.fact: Won its qualifying group without conceding a single goal — fitting for the first African nation ever to win a World Cup match, against Mexico in 1978.

Ivory Coast.name: Ivory Coast
Ivory Coast.fact: Arrives as the reigning African champion — they won the 2023 AFCON on home soil after nearly going out in the group stage.

Ghana.name: Ghana
Ghana.fact: Its best World Cup run remains the 2010 quarter-finals — one Luis Suárez handball on the line away from Africa's first ever semi-final. Never forgiven, never forgotten.

South Africa.name: South Africa
South Africa.fact: Faces Mexico in the tournament's opening match at the Azteca — their first World Cup since hosting in 2010.

Cape Verde.name: Cape Verde
Cape Verde.fact: Making its World Cup debut — an island nation of barely half a million people, the smallest African country ever to qualify.

DR Congo.name: DR Congo
DR Congo.fact: Returns for the first time since playing as Zaire in 1974 — a 52-year wait ended by an extra-time playoff winner against Jamaica.

Panama.name: Panama
Panama.fact: Back after its World Cup debut in 2018, when they scored their first ever World Cup goal — and the whole country celebrated like they'd won the thing.

Curacao.name: Curaçao
Curacao.fact: Debutant and the smallest nation by population ever at a World Cup — a Caribbean island of about 156,000 that went unbeaten through CONCACAF qualifying. Fewer residents than Walthamstow.

Haiti.name: Haiti
Haiti.fact: An emotional return after winning its qualifying group ahead of Honduras and Costa Rica — their first World Cup since 1974, when they famously took the lead against mighty Italy.

New Zealand.name: New Zealand
New Zealand.fact: The first Oceania team ever to earn a direct, automatic qualifying berth. At their last World Cup in 2010 they drew all three games and went home as the only unbeaten team in the tournament. Peak Kiwi.
